audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar Levite-city and refuge-city law tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for forty-eight Levite cities, pasture measurements, six refuge cities, unintentional versus intentional killing, blood-avenger boundaries, congregational judgment, high-priest death, witness requirements, ransom prohibition, and land-blood theology. Strong due-process and anti-death-penalty safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses in plains of Moab by Jordan at Jericho, saying,
Command children of Israel that they give to Levites from inheritance of their possession cities to dwell in, and pastureland around cities you shall give to Levites.
Cities shall be for them to dwell in, and their pasturelands shall be for their cattle, their property, and all their animals.
Pasturelands of cities that you give to Levites shall be from wall of city outward one thousand cubits around.

You shall measure outside city on east side two thousand cubits, south side two thousand cubits, west side two thousand cubits, and north side two thousand cubits, with city in midst; this shall be for them pasturelands of cities.
Cities that you give to Levites shall include six cities of refuge, which you shall give for manslayer to flee there; and besides them you shall give forty-two cities.
All cities you shall give to Levites shall be forty-eight cities, them and their pasturelands.

Cities you give from possession of children of Israel: from many you shall take many and from few you shall take few; each according to inheritance that it inherits shall give from its cities to Levites.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel and say to them: When you cross Jordan into land of Canaan,
you shall designate for yourselves cities, cities of refuge shall they be for you, and a manslayer who strikes a soul/life unintentionally may flee there.
Cities shall be for you as refuge from blood-avenger, so manslayer does not die until he stands before congregation for judgment.
Cities that you give shall be six cities of refuge for you.
Three cities you shall give across Jordan and three cities you shall give in land of Canaan; cities of refuge they shall be.
For children of Israel, for sojourner, and for resident settler in their midst these six cities shall be refuge, for everyone who strikes a soul/life unintentionally to flee there.
But if with instrument of iron he struck him and he died, a murderer he is; murderer shall surely be put to death.
If with stone in hand by which one could die he struck him and he died, a murderer he is; murderer shall surely be put to death.
Or if with instrument of wood in hand by which one could die he struck him and he died, a murderer he is; murderer shall surely be put to death.
Blood-avenger himself shall put murderer to death; when he meets him, he shall put him to death.
If in hatred he pushes him or throws at him while lying in wait, and he dies,
or in enmity strikes him with his hand and he dies, the one who struck shall surely be put to death; a murderer he is. Blood-avenger shall put murderer to death when he meets him.
But if suddenly without enmity he pushed him, or threw any object upon him without lying in wait,
or with any stone by which one could die, without seeing, caused it to fall upon him and he died, though he was not his enemy and did not seek his harm,
then congregation shall judge between one who struck and blood-avenger according to these judgments.

Congregation shall rescue manslayer from hand of blood-avenger, and congregation shall return him to city of refuge to which he fled; he shall dwell in it until death of high priest who was anointed with holy oil.
But if manslayer goes out at all beyond border of his city of refuge to which he fled,
and blood-avenger finds him outside border of his city of refuge, and blood-avenger kills manslayer, there is no bloodguilt for him.
For in his city of refuge he shall dwell until death of high priest; after death of high priest manslayer may return to land of his possession.
These shall be for you as statute of judgment throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
Everyone striking a soul/life: by mouth of witnesses murderer shall be put to death; but one witness shall not answer against a soul/life for death.
You shall not take ransom for soul/life of murderer who is guilty to die, but he shall surely be put to death.
You shall not take ransom for one who fled to his city of refuge, to return to dwell in land before death of priest.
You shall not pollute land where you are, for blood pollutes land; and for land no atonement/covering can be made for blood shed in it except by blood of one who shed it.
You shall not defile land in which you dwell, in whose midst I dwell; for I YHWH dwell in midst of children of Israel.

translation_notes: Cities of refuge interrupt immediate blood vengeance and require communal judgment before death can occur.
The asylum system is open to Israelite, sojourner, and resident settler alike.
The law distinguishes intent, hatred, ambush, weapon, accident, and prior relationship; this is an ancient due-process development rather than permission for impulsive retaliation.
One witness is insufficient for a death sentence, preserving a crucial evidentiary boundary.
Residence until the high priest's death is protective exile but also a major restriction on an unintentional killer and requires legal-history critique.
The blood-avenger and execution provisions are source-preserved ancient law and must not authorize private vengeance, capital punishment, or modern vigilantism.
Verse 33's blood-atonement claim must not be used to demand executions; modern justice can protect life through due process, prevention, accountability, and nonlethal means.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 35 to justify capital punishment, honor revenge, family vengeance, vigilantism, or killing outside lawful process. Its enduring ethical movement is away from immediate retaliation and toward refuge, evidence, intent analysis, equal access, communal judgment, and protection of life.
